algorithm_input.title: NCARB-derived event venue voice-alarm channel precedence
algorithm_input.design_intent: Paraphrased NCARB-derived scenario: restaurant event configuration and alarm priority. The optimizer uses fixed source-grounded code semantics while tuning visible design parameters and public scenario-model trade-offs.

scenario_facts.ncarb_scenario_theme: restaurant event configuration and alarm priority
scenario_facts.scenario_variant_differentiator: Event venue voice channel capacity variant.

query_preferences.meaning: allow event-venue operator flexibility while keeping alarm priority

public_scenario_model.purpose: Non-normative task physics/objective-closure constraints visible to optimizers; contains no expected rule IDs or labels.

algorithm_input.visible_input_note: Visible task input only. Algorithms may also read the public scenario model and rule library. Rule labels, rule-derived feasible-region answers, and rule-id bindings remain hidden evaluation references.

source_task_origin.ncarb_scenario_theme: restaurant event configuration and alarm priority

algorithm_input.scenario_variant_note: Parameterized variant with substantive changes in facts, bounds, weights, or document context; not a fully independent real project case.
algorithm_input.task_semantics_note: Rule applicability, precedence, categorical, or system-capability semantics dominate this task; it is not a pure continuous numeric optimization task.
algorithm_input.scenario_variant_differentiator: Event venue voice channel capacity variant.
